content: Instead of relying on large language model chain-of-thought or fixed tool-calling logic, we can treat every interaction with an AI agent as a case matching problem: the agent retrieves the most behaviorally and contextually similar past agent trajectories via cosine similarity, then adapts that entire trajectory to the current query rather than planning from scratch. This creates "behavioral memory" that lets AI agents improve efficiency by leaning on proven past action sequences rather than re-computing plans for every new input.
